result.summary: Current-head evidence is fresh enough for this gate-evidence role, focused command evidence is reliable, and gate binding correctly keeps PR creation blocked until the review lifecycle is recorded.
result.inspection_summary: Inspected the requested review inputs, preferred evidence artifacts, bounded summaries before over-budget artifacts, PR/gate binding outputs, source/test diffs, verification evidence, local git head, and reran the focused tests. Mandatory regression_guard and path_surface_coverage lenses both pass for the reviewed concern.
judgment_delta: evidence freshness concern -> pass because evidence-reuse reports evidence_key_match, stale_reasons=[], fresh_use_allowed=true, current_head_bound artifacts, and local HEAD equals ccea8bb668539ee9c36df41b4d7bef341348d0d3.
command reliability concern -> pass because recorded typecheck, integration, unit, and focused e2e evidence are strict-head bound, and the focused tests were rerun successfully during this review.

gate binding concern -> pass for this role because unresolved agent-review and senior-gap gates are explicit in decision-index/pr-prepare and PR create remains blocked; that is the expected pre-record state for this subagent result.
regression_guard lens -> pass because existing audit session-cost automation-memory behavior, token accounting, docs-only axis suppression, and current-head focused regression coverage are verified.

path_surface_coverage lens -> pass because missing/corrupt memory, explicit fallback options, valid memory, commit readback, free-form memory preservation, CLI exit code behavior, continuity artifacts, PR body, traceability, and bounded summary surfaces are represented.

managed worktree locality warning -> non-blocking because the recorded warning is preferred/not required, actual repo root matches the requested worktree, and current_head_sha matches expected_head_sha.
